caribbean.headline: Roatan faces heavy rain and moderate seas today
caribbean.paragraph: Roatan is experiencing 23-knot winds with 5.8-foot significant wave heights and an 8-second swell period from the ENE, creating moderate sea conditions for transit and port operations. An 88% rain probability means substantial precipitation is expected throughout the day, which will limit visibility and require standard weather precautions for all vessel movements in the Western Caribbean.

mediterranean.headline: Mediterranean Moderate Seas Expected as Mykonos Faces Elevated Conditions
mediterranean.paragraph: Mykonos is experiencing 22 kt winds with significant wave heights reaching 5.6 ft and a 6 second northeast swell period, making this the most impactful weather story across the Mediterranean today. Less than 10% rain probability keeps precipitation concerns minimal throughout the region, while Venice to the west remains relatively benign with only 12 kt winds.
